channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from trusted sources

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel-Hezbollah Exchange Fire Despite Trump Ceasefire Announcement
item.description: Israel conducted strikes on south Lebanon and Hezbollah fired into northern Israel after Trump announced a ceasefire agreement that neither side has publicly accepted. The violence threatens to derail US-Iran peace negotiations as Tehran said it was no longer engaging in talks due to Israels offensive.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine Senate Deadlock Deepens After Estrada Arrest
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estradas arrest on corruption charges has left the Senate split between two 11-member blocs, raising risks of legislative paralysis and complicating Vice-President Sara Dutertes impeachment trial. The standoff follows Senator Dela Rosas disappearance after an ICC arrest warrant.

item.title: ✈️ Boeing Validates MQ-28 Ghost Bat Stealth Performance in RCS Testing
item.description: Boeing confirmed the MQ-28 Ghost Bat collaborative combat aircrafts stealth capabilities through radar cross section testing, validating design and material choices that minimize radar detection. The aircraft has completed over 150 flights and is designed for surveillance, electronic warfare, and force multiplication.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Attacks Kill 13 Across Ukraine in Major Overnight Strike
item.description: Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukrainian cities including Kyiv, Dnipro, and Kharkiv, killing at least 13 and wounding over 100. The attack included eight Zircon hypersonic missiles, likely the largest number used in a single strike during the war.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Russia Launches Major Attack on Ukraine, Killing at Least 11
item.description: Russia launched one of its largest air assaults on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities in months, firing 73 missiles and 656 drones. At least 11 people were killed and over 100 wounded in the overnight strikes, which Russia called a response to Kyivs terrorist acts inside Russia.

item.title: 🇺🇦🚢🔥 Ukrainian Drone Attack Sets Russian Oil Refinery on Fire
item.description: Ukraine continued its campaign against Russian energy infrastructure as the Ilsky oil refinery in Russias Krasnodar region caught fire after an overnight drone attack. Kyiv has been systematically targeting Russian oil facilities to starve Moscow of war revenue.

item.title: 🇪🇺🏛️ EU Agrees Major Migration Overhaul with Offshore Return Centers
item.description: The European Union has advanced a sweeping migration policy reform that would increase deportations and establish detention centers abroad. The deal represents a significant shift toward tougher border controls as the bloc responds to political pressure over migration.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇦🇲 Russia Increases Pressure on Armenia Ahead of Election
item.description: Russia is intensifying pressure on Armenia ahead of a June 7 election as the once-close ally pursues deeper ties with Brussels and Washington. The move reflects Moscows efforts to maintain influence in its traditional sphere amid Armenias Western pivot.

item.title: 🛡️💰 Motorola Solutions Buys Israeli Drone Defense Startup for $1.5 Billion
item.description: Motorola Solutions announced it will acquire Israeli startup D-Fend Solutions for $1.5 billion, highlighting growing demand for counter-drone technology as governments and critical infrastructure operators worldwide face increasing threats from rogue drones.

item.title: ☢️ US in talks to expand nuclear weapons deployments in Europe
item.description: The US is discussing whether to deploy nuclear weapons in additional European NATO states, the Financial Times reported. The discussions come amid heightened tensions with Russia and growing concerns about European security.

item.title: 🚢 US crude exports hit record 5.6 million barrels per day in May
item.description: US crude exports climbed to a record high as the Middle East crisis pushed up demand from Asian and European refiners. The surge reflects global supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

item.title: 🏰 Israeli forces seize 900-year-old Crusader castle in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli troops entered a medieval stronghold built by Crusaders in southern Lebanon, capturing a site with deep historical significance. The move is part of Israel's ongoing operations against Hezbollah positions.

item.title: 🇫🇷🍇🌳 French winemakers embrace vitiforestry to combat climate change
item.description: Winegrowers across France are experimenting with growing trees alongside vines, reviving an ancient practice to protect grapes from heatwaves, drought and increasingly violent weather patterns driven by climate change.

item.title: 🇲🇾🇳🇴🚀 Malaysia demands Norway expedite refund for cancelled missile deal
item.description: Malaysia's Defense Minister urged Norway to speed up the refund for a cancelled Naval Strike Missile system after Norway refused to approve the export licence. Malaysia is now exploring alternatives from Italy, France and South Korea.

item.title: 🇵🇭📉 Philippine President Marcos approval rating hits record low
item.description: Ferdinand Marcos Jr's net satisfaction rating fell to minus 15, the worst of his presidency, as inflation driven by the Iran war and economic anxieties continue to bite. His rating collapsed across all regions, with Mindanao hitting minus 40.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚠️ Iran Suspends US Negotiations Over Israeli Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: Iran stops diplomatic message exchanges with the US through mediators, citing continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon. Tehran warns it may block the Strait of Hormuz as the three-month conflict continues.

item.title: 🇹🇼🤖 Taiwan Develops Robot Patrol Dogs for South China Sea Islands
item.description: Taiwan's military weapons institute reveals robot patrol dogs for potential deployment on disputed South China Sea islands, as Chinese coast guard patrols intensify near Taiwanese-held territories.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China and US Hold Constructive Military Talks in Hawaii
item.description: Chinese and US military officials met in Hawaii for candid discussions on air and maritime safety, agreeing that improved communication could reduce miscalculations and enhance professionalism amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇫🇷💰 France Secures €93 Billion in Foreign Investments at Choose France Summit
item.description: France announces 71 foreign investment projects worth €93 billion ($108.3 billion) at its annual business summit, creating over 15,600 jobs as Macron courts global investors.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Agrees to Ceasefire With Israel, Trump Announces
item.description: Lebanon confirmed Hezbollah accepted a US proposal for a mutual cessation of attacks, with Israel agreeing not to strike Beirut. However, clashes continued hours after the announcement, with Hezbollah launching attacks on Israeli tanks. Iran warned that violations in Lebanon would breach the broader US-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Macau joins China cross-border digital currency platform as 11 banks sign up
item.description: Macau has joined Project mBridge, a multi-country central bank digital currency settlement platform alongside China, Hong Kong, Thailand, UAE and Saudi Arabia. The move advances Beijing's push to create alternatives to US dollar-centric payment networks amid heightened tensions.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Chinese automaker SAIC to build first EU car plant in Spain
item.description: SAIC Motor plans to establish its first European Union production facility in Spain's Galicia region with a €200 million initial investment. The MG brand owner aims to start construction in 2027 and begin operations by 2028, creating approximately 1,000 direct jobs.

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark's Frederiksen secures third term amid Greenland tensions with Trump
item.description: Danish PM Mette Frederiksen formed a center-left minority coalition government after more than two months of negotiations following March elections. She maintains power during a diplomatic crisis with the US over the future of Greenland.

item.title: 🚢 China advances fourth aircraft carrier as powers invest despite drone threat
item.description: Satellite images show China's fourth aircraft carrier, the nuclear-powered Type 004, taking shape at Dalian shipyard with electromagnetic catapults. Despite growing drone and submarine threats to large surface vessels, major powers including France, Turkey, India and Japan continue investing in carriers as symbols of military might.
